coredata.dc:title: Effective protection of high-performance dopamine-based corrosion inhibitor on metal surface
coredata.dc:creator: Xie, Haolin
Xiao, Mengxue
Zhang, Fan
Ouyang, Shuilin
Ye, Y.W.
Chen, H.
coredata.dc:description: To improve the service life of the Q235 steel in harsh environments, a high-performance inhibitor of modified dopamine was successfully synthesized between dopamine and polyethylene glycol diacrylate. The electrochemical behaviors of Q235 steel in 1 M HCl and 3.5% NaCl solutions with and without inhibitor were evaluated at 298 K. The result revealed that the addition of modified dopamine inhibitor could be effectively restrain the corrosion of Q235 steel. Simultaneously, when the modified dopamine inhibitor was 100 mg/L, the corrosion current density (i corr) of Q235 steel in 1 M HCl and 3.5% NaCl solutions decreased by 16 and 24 times, respectively. At this case, the corrosion inhibition efficiency (IE) increased from 59.1% to 69.2%–93.7% and 96.5% in 1 M HCl and 3.5% NaCl solutions when the inhibitor was changed from primordial dopamine (PD) to modified dopamine (MD), respectively. The improvement of these properties was due to the high coverage and strong adsorption of modified dopamine on steel surface.
coredata.dcterms:subject: Dopamine
EIS
Tafel curve
Inhibition efficiency
Acid and neutral environments

xocs:meta.xocs:articleinfo: articleinfo articlenumber articletitlenorm authfirstinitialnorm authfirstsurnamenorm cid cids contenttype copyright crossmark dateloaded dateloadedtxt datesearch datesort dateupdated dco docsubtype doctype doi eid ewtransactionid hubeid indexeddate issn issnnorm itemstage itemtransactionid itemweight openaccess openarchive pg pgfirst pii piinorm pubdatestart pubdatetxt pubyr sectiontitle sortorder srctitle srctitlenorm srctype ssids alllist content subj subheadings suppl tomb volfirst volissue volumelist webpdf webpdfpagecount yearnav figure table e-component body mmlmath acknowledge affil appendices articletitle auth authfirstini authfull authkeywords authlast footnotes grantnumber grantsponsor grantsponsorid highlightsabst orcid primabst ref

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: Synthetic process of modified dopamine inhibitor.
OCP curves of steel in acid and neutral solutions with various inhibitors (a) primordial dopamine-HCl, (b) primordial dopamine-NaCl, (c) modified dopamine-HCl, (d) modified dopamine-NaCl.
EIS results of steel in different solutions (a) (b) primordial dopamine-HCl, (c) (d) primordial dopamine-NaCl, (e) (f) modified dopamine-HCl, (g) (h) modified dopamine-NaCl.
Electrical equivalent circuit fitted by EIS data.

The variation of Nyquist diagrams and Bode plots as a function of immersion time (a) (b) primordial dopamine-HCl, (c) (d) primordial dopamine-NaCl, (e) (f) modified dopamine-HCl, (g) (h) modified dopamine-NaCl.

Potentiodynamic polarization plots of steel after 8 h immersion in acid and neutral solutions with various inhibitors (a) primordial dopamine-HCl, (b) primordial dopamine-NaCl, (c) modified dopamine-HCl, (d) modified dopamine-NaCl.
2D surface morphology and chemical composition of the immersed bare steel.
3D surface morphology and roughness of the immersed bare steel in various conditions.
Corrosion rate of bare steel after immersion in various conditions (a) primordial dopamine and (b) modified dopamine.
Adsorption isotherm of bare steel after immersion in various conditions (a) primordial dopamine-HCl, (b) primordial dopamine-NaCl, (c) modified dopamine-HCl and (d) modified dopamine-NaCl.
Adsorption and anti-corrosion mechanism of modified dopamine for steel.
The EIS fitting parameters of steel in primordial dopamine solution.
The EIS fitting parameters of steel in modified dopamine solution.
The tafel fitting parameters of steel in primordial dopamine solution.
The tafel fitting parameters of steel in modified dopamine solution.

head.ce:title: Effective protection of high-performance dopamine-based corrosion inhibitor on metal surface

ce:affiliation.ce:label: a
ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: Faculty of Materials Metallurgy and Chemistry, Jiangxi University of Science and Technology, Ganzhou, 341000, China

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: Faculty of Materials Metallurgy and Chemistry
Jiangxi University of Science and Technology

ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: Faculty of Materials Metallurgy and Chemistry, Jiangxi University of Science and Technology, Ganzhou, 341000, China

ce:affiliation.ce:label: b
ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: College of Rear Earth, Jiangxi University of Science and Technology, Ganzhou, 341000, China

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: Jiangxi University of Science and Technology

ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: College of Rear Earth, Jiangxi University of Science and Technology, Ganzhou, 341000, China

ce:footnote.ce:note-para: These authors contribute equally to this work.

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: To improve the service life of the Q235 steel in harsh environments, a high-performance inhibitor of modified dopamine was successfully synthesized between dopamine and polyethylene glycol diacrylate. The electrochemical behaviors of Q235 steel in 1 M HCl and 3.5% NaCl solutions with and without inhibitor were evaluated at 298 K. The result revealed that the addition of modified dopamine inhibitor could be effectively restrain the corrosion of Q235 steel. Simultaneously, when the modified dopamine inhibitor was 100 mg/L, the corrosion current density (i corr) of Q235 steel in 1 M HCl and 3.5% NaCl solutions decreased by 16 and 24 times, respectively. At this case, the corrosion inhibition efficiency (IE) increased from 59.1% to 69.2%–93.7% and 96.5% in 1 M HCl and 3.5% NaCl solutions when the inhibitor was changed from primordial dopamine (PD) to modified dopamine (MD), respectively. The improvement of these properties was due to the high coverage and strong adsorption of modified dopamine on steel surface.

ce:data-availability.ce:para: The data that has been used is confidential.

ce:section.ce:para: Due to its excellent mechanical and casting properties, metals account for the largest proportion in material applications, reaching more than 90%. Such as metal products, precision instruments, electronic equipment, thermal equipment, petrochemical equipment, navigation and aerospace equipment, ordnance equipment and other fields, metal has a wide range of applications [1]. However, metals are easy to lose electrons and become their corresponding ions in many environments, leading to corrosion. The direct and indirect losses caused by corrosion are staggering. In my country, the loss only caused by steel corrosion accounted for 5% of the gross national product [2]. More importantly, the application of high technology and new materials and the progress of major projects are hindered by the problem of metal corrosion [3,4]. Therefore, in recent decades, many scientific researchers have focused on the study of metal corrosion and anticorrosion, and developed a series of metal corrosion inhibitors, also known as corrosion inhibitors.

In general, corrosion inhibitor is a substance that can form an adsorption layer on the metal surface, thereby shielding the corrosive medium in the environment and inhibiting the electrochemical reaction of metal corrosion [5,6]. Some traditional high-efficiency corrosion inhibitors, such as heavy metal salts, are difficult to be biodegraded or even toxic, which will cause serious environmental pollution problems. Therefore, it is necessary to develop new environment-friendly corrosion inhibitors to replace traditional inhibitors [7–12]. Polymer corrosion inhibitors have attracted extensive attention from researchers due to its superior comprehensive properties. For instance, Mobin et al. [13] studied the corrosion inhibition performance of polysaccharides on carbon steel in 1 mol/L HCl solution, and found that the corrosion inhibition rate could reach 92.5% at a high concentration (1000 mg L−1). l-histidine and l-arginine modified konjac glucomannan were also used as corrosion inhibitors for carbon steel in acidic medium. When the concentration was 2000 mg L−1, the corrosion inhibition rates were about 89.9% and 92.4%, respectively [14]. The research results of Sundaram et al. [15] showed that the corrosion inhibition rate of carbon steel in 1 mol/L HCl solution with 900 mg L−1 of aminated hydroxyethyl cellulose exceeded 90%, but the corrosion inhibition rate was only about 40% at low concentration (100 mg L−1). In addition, starch and chitosan derivatives also have a certain corrosion inhibition performance for metals in acidic medium [16,17]. But generally speaking, these polymer corrosion inhibitors need to reach a high concentration to achieve a high corrosion inhibition rate. Therefore, the modification on the basis of polymer can maintain high corrosion inhibition efficiency under the premise of low inhibitor concentration.

Dopamine is used in the anti-corrosion field because of its special structure and good properties. Haruna et al. [18] obtained a corrosion inhibitor between graphene oxide and dopamine, and found that the IE of metal in HCl solution with 5 ppm inhibitor was up to 91.1%. Yang et al. [19] selected polydopamine as a inhibitor for mild steel in HCl solution, and pointed out that the IE of steel in 40 mol/L inhibitor solution was about 86%. Cui et al. [20] successfully prepared the dopamine-produced carbon dot inhibitor, and revealed that the IE of steel in 3.5% NaCl solution with 100 ppm inhibitor reached 88.8%. Chai et al. [21] prepared a green inhibitor of dopamine-modified polyaspartic acid and discussed the corrosion behavior of steel in H2SO4 solution. By analysis, the dopamine could effectively enhance the inhibition rate of metal in corrosion environment. Wang et al. [22] grafted dopamine to the surface of metal-organic frameworks (MOFs) and then doped into epoxy matrix. By electrochemical test, the addition of dopamine enhanced the water resistance and corrosion resistance of coating. After that, they used dopamine to modify the mesoporous-TiO2 particles and found that the salt spray resistance was improved by the unique network structure caused by dopamine [23]. Zhou et al. [24] used 5β-TCP/Mg–3Zn to coat dopamine-gelatin composite and pointed out that the corrosion current density of metal after coverage was greatly reduced compared with bare metal.

In this work, dopamine was chosen to modify the polyethylene glycol diacrylate to form a high-performance inhibitor. Combined with statistics and analysis, the as-prepared inhibitor has the advantages of low cost and environmental friendliness. According to the electrochemical testing, the as-prepared inhibitor showed higher corrosion inhibition efficiency for metal protection in acidic and neutral environments compared with other inhibitor in Table S1 [18–21,25–32].

Dopamine and polyethylene glycol diacrylate were obtained from Qingyun Science and Education Equipment Co., Ltd. Deionized water was prepared by corresponding equipment. The Q235 steel (10 mm × 10 mm × 10 mm) was purchased from Shengxing Technology Co., Ltd. The dialysis bag, sodium chloride and hydrochloric acid reagents were purchased on Taobao mall.

The synthetic process of modified dopamine inhibitor was displayed in Fig. 1 . 0.5 mol of dopamine and 20 ml of deionized water were uniformly mixed by mechanical agitation and ultrasonic treatment to obtain a reaction precursor solution. The agitation treatment time was 15 min, and the sonication treatment time was 20 min. After that, 0.5 mol of polyethylene glycol diacrylate was slowly added to the above-mentioned reaction precursor solution in water bath equipment. The obtained mixture was stirred for 24 h at a room temperature to conduct polycondensation to form the polymer modified dopamine corrosion inhibitor. Then, the obtained reaction solution was subjected to filtration treatment, dialysis treatment and drying treatment in this order. The molecular weight of the dialysis bag used in the dialysis treatment was 1000. The drying temperature was 80 °C, and the drying time was 24 h. By evaluating, the solubility of as-obtained inhibitor in distilled water was about 10 mg/ml. In order to confirm the successful reaction, the 1H NMR spectrum of as-prepared inhibitor was shown in Fig. S1.

The chemical structure of modified dopamine inhibitor was investigated through FTIR and NMR. The corrosion behaviors of steel in 1 M HCl and 3.5% NaCl solution with 0, 10, 30, 50, 100 mg/L of as-prepared inhibitor were carried out on a CH660 electrochemical workstation in ambient air at 298 K. Among them, the traditional three-electrode electrochemical cell including a working electrode (steel), a platinum plate electrode and a saturated calomel electrode was selected. Before EIS and tafel tests, the OCP value was measured for 10 min to obtain a stable result. After that, the EIS data was conducted in the frequency range of 105 Hz to 10−1 Hz with sinusoidal amplitude of 20 mV. The tafel curve was obtained at 1 mV/s within±250 mV versus EOCP. Weight loss test was conducted according to the NACE TM0169/G31 [33]. After these tests, SEM was used to observe the 2D corrosion morphology and LSCM was conducted to obtain the 3D surface morphology.

The FTIR result was displayed in Fig. 2 . Some peaks were detected at 1628.1, 3401.1, 1117.8, 1589.1, 2987.2 cm−1, corresponding to CC and C–OH in benzene ring, C–N, benzene ring, C–H groups, respectively (Fig. 2a). These peaks were from primordial dopamine. Meanwhile, the peaks located at 1104.9 and 1722.2 cm−1 were related to -C-O-C- and CO groups, which came from polyethylene glycol diacrylate. Two special peaks at 1384.9 and 3371.2 cm−1 were observed on the spectrum, which was attributed to the vibration of C–N framework and N–H, implying the successfully reaction between primordial dopamine and polyethylene glycol diacrylate [34].

Fig. 3 exhibited the OCP curves of steel in acid and neutral solutions with various inhibitors. As a whole, the stable OCP value of steel shifted to the negative direction in HCl solution and transferred to the positive direction in NaCl solution with the increase of inhibitor content, implying that the as-prepared inhibitor could restrain the corrosion reaction in different environments [35]. At the same parameter, the stable OCP value of steel in HCl solution with modified dopamine was lower than that with primordial dopamine. However, the change trend was opposite in NaCl solution. For instance, the stable OCP value of steel in neutral solution with 100 mg/L of modified dopamine was −0.571 V vs SCE and the value was −0.602 V vs SCE in 100 mg/L of primordial dopamine solution. However, the stable OCP value of steel in HCl solution with 100 mg/L of modified dopamine was −0.496 V vs SCE, and the value of steel in 100 mg/L of primordial dopamine solution reached −0.475 V vs SCE. Moreover, compared with the blank solution, the change degree of OCP value in neutral environment was obviously higher than that in acid environment, suggesting the high sensitivity in neutral solution.

The corrosion behavior of Q235 steel samples in acid and neutral environments with and without inhibitor was observed by EIS in Fig. 4 . Large capacitance arc corresponded to high corrosion resistance [36]. Obviously, high inhibitor concentration promoted the expansion of capacitive arc. Through observation, the capacitive arc in neutral environment was larger than that in acid environment at the same condition. In the meantime, the capacitive arc in modified dopamine solution was greater than that in primordial dopamine solution. The low-frequency region was related to the polarization resistance and the |Z|0.1Hz could reflect the inhibition ability [37,38]. From image, the |Z|0.1Hz in blank acid solution was close to 33 Ω cm2. After adding 100 mg/L of primordial dopamine, the |Z|0.1Hz went up to 72 Ω cm2, showing a slight inhibition effect. After modification, the |Z|0.1Hz changed from 850 to 1500 Ω cm2 when the modified dopamine increased from 10 to 100 mg/L. Compared with primordial dopamine, the |Z|0.1Hz value was increased by two order of magnitudes, indicating a good protective effect for steel. In neutral solution, the |Z|0.1Hz value was about 120 Ω cm2 in blank solution. As the increase of primordial dopamine, the |Z|0.1Hz value continued to rise and reached 800 Ω cm2 at 100 mg/L. However, the |Z|0.1Hz value was 2500 Ω cm2 after adding 100 mg/L of modified dopamine, which was 1.7 times higher than that in acidic condition. These EIS results revealed that the addition of modified dopamine in acid and neutral solutions could sharply reinforce the protective ability of metal.

According to the above results, the EIS parameters were fitted via the equivalent circuit model in Fig. 5 . Among all parameters, R s was the solution resistance, R f and CPE f were related to the film resistance and constant phase element of film, R ct and CPE dl represented the charge transfer resistance and constant phase element of double layer, respectively [39]. The fitting results were shown in Table 1 and Table 2 . R f and R ct values were two important parameters to evaluate the corrosion process. Clearly, both R f and R ct values increased along with the inhibitor amount increase, manifesting that the protective ability was enhanced by dopamine-based inhibitor. Furthermore, the change of R f and R ct values could indirectly reflect the increase in the adsorption film thickness and the inhibitor coverage area. Through comparison, the R f and R ct values in modified dopamine solution were higher than that in primordial dopamine solution, indicating that the modified dopamine was easier to adsorb on metal surface. Meanwhile, the R f and R ct values in neutral solution were obviously higher than that in acid solution, showing that a neutral environment was more conducive to the inhibition effect of modified dopamine.

The impedance change in different immersion time was also the key to estimate the modified dopamine. In Fig. 6 , the long immersion time promoted the expansion of capacitive arc, which was due to the formation of a thin layer containing corrosion inhibitor on metal surface. Meanwhile, the capacitive arc in neutral environment was also larger than that in acid environment, and the modified dopamine could more effectively increase the arc diameter of capacitive compared with primordial dopamine. By calculation, the |Z|0.1Hz value in acid environment with 100 mg/L of primordial dopamine increased from 40 to 70 Ω cm2 when the immersion time changed from 2 to 8 h, which was increased by 75%. In neutral solution, the |Z|0.1Hz value of steel changed from 400 to 800 Ω cm2 in corresponding time range, which was doubled. In the case of modified dopamine, the |Z|0.1Hz value in acid environment increased from 1080 to 1500 Ω cm2 with time. However, the |Z|0.1Hz value was about 1500–2500 Ω cm2 with the change of immersion time, which was drastically higher than that in acid solution and primordial dopamine solution. This was an impressive result compared with the well-known inhibitors until yet.

The fitting parameters were listed in Table 2. From 2 to 8 h, the R f and R ct values in both environments increased, implying that the long immersion time promoted the expansion of adsorption film thickness and coverage area. For example, the R f and R ct values of steel in acid solution with 100 mg/L of primordial dopamine after 8 h immersion were about 20.06 and 71.65 Ω cm2, which was 81.82% and 93.91% higher than 2 h immersion. In neutral solution, the ratio values were increased to 138.93% and 105.71%, respectively. For modified dopamine, the R f and R ct values were obviously enhanced compared with primordial dopamine. By analysis, the R f and R ct values of steel in acid solution after 8 h immersion were about 48.93 and 1452.66 Ω cm2, which were 2.43 and 20.27 times higher than primordial dopamine at the same condition. In neutral condition, although the increase ratio was not as high as that in acidic environment, the specific impedance value was much higher than that in acidic environment.

Fig. 7 illustrated the potentiodynamic polarization plots of steel after 8 h immersion in acid and neutral solutions with various inhibitors. According to the result, the inhibitor drastically suppressed the anodic and cathodic reactions. The alteration of Tafel branches indicated the influence of inhibitor on corrosion reaction. The influence of inhibitor type and corrosion medium was closely related to the Tafel branches. After statistics, some fitted parameters were listed Table 3 and Table 4 . They were corrosion potential, corrosion current density, anode slope, cathode slope, polarization resistance, surface coverage and inhibition efficiency from left to right. By observation, the E corr was decreased in acid environment and increased in neutral environment after adding as-prepared samples. Nevertheless, the i corr of steel decreased in both environments as inhibitor amount increased. For example, the i corr of steel decreased from 314.8 to 106.9 μA cm−2 to 128.8 and 32.9 μA cm−2 in acid and neutral solutions after adding 100 mg/L of inhibitor, respectively. In terms of inhibitor type, the reduction degree of i corr caused by modified dopamine was larger than that caused by primordial dopamine. For corrosion environment, the inhibition effect of both inhibitors in neutral solution was stronger than that in acid solution at the same condition. Especially for modified dopamine in neutral solution, the i corr value at 100 mg/L was two orders of magnitude lower in comparison with blank solution.

The polarization resistance was the authoritative physical quantity to measure the corrosion resistance of materials. Obviously, the polarization resistance of steel in blank acid and neutral solutions was about 60.7 and 294.7 Ω cm2. After adding inhibitor, the polarization resistance was positively correlated with the content of inhibitor, implying that both inhibitors could enhance the protection ability for bare steel. When the content of primordial dopamine was 100 mg/L, the polarization resistance was about 339.7 and 828.1 Ω cm2 in acid and neutral solutions. Nevertheless, the value was increased by 1624.2 and 2239.8 Ω cm2 after adding 100 mg/L of modified dopamine in corresponding solution, which was 4.78 and 2.70 times higher than primordial dopamine. The surface coverage was calculated from equation (1) [40]:
(1)
θ =
i
c o r r
0
−
i
c o r r
i
c o r r
0

Among them, i 0 corr was the current density in blank solution and i corr was the current density after adding inhibitor. In general, the larger the surface coverage is, the broader the protection area is. This is because the coverage can block the contact of corrosive medium with the metal substrate and avoid the occurrence of corrosion. From the result, the surface coverage increased with the increase of inhibitor addition amount, indicating that the metal surface was covered by inhibitor. By contrast, the max surface coverage of primordial dopamine in acid and neutral solutions was 0.591 and 0.692, which was significantly lower than that of modified dopamine (0.937 and 0.965) in corresponding environment. The data implied that the modified dopamine was easier to cover the metal surface in comparison with primordial dopamine. The inhibition efficiency was obtained from equation (2) [41]:
(2)
I E =
i
c o r r
0
−
i
c o r r
i
c o r r
0
× 100 %

The same as surface coverage, the inhibition efficiency was also positively related to the inhibitor addition amount. For primordial dopamine, the inhibition efficiency increased from 7.6% to 34.8%–59.1% and 69.2% in acid and neutral solutions when the addition amount increased from 10 to 100 mg/L. However, the values increased from 87.5% to 37.9%–93.7% and 96.5% in acid and neutral solutions after adding modified dopamine. By comparison, the inhibition effect of modified dopamine was better than that of primordial dopamine. Moreover, the IE of modified dopamine in acid solution was higher than that of other corrosion inhibitors in Table S1, such as GO grafted with dopamine (91.1%) [18], dopamine-modified polyaspartic acid (90.9%) [21], polydopamine (86%) [19], N-doped carbon dots (88.9%) [25], Rollinia occidentalis extract (85.1%) [26], Konjac glucomannan (92.4%) [27], Pyridazinium ionic liquids (84.0%) [28]. Meanwhile, the IE of modified dopamine in 3.5% NaCl solution was also higher than that of GO grafted with dopamine (87.1%) [29], dopamine-produced carbon dots (88.8%) [20], poly (dopamine) modified h-BN (86%) [30], N-doped carbon dots (88.9%) [25], β-CD with dopamine (85%) [31] and dopamine wrapped with MWCNTs (86.7%) [32].

Fig. 8 displayed the 2D surface morphology and chemical composition of bare steel after immersion. In neutral environment, a coarse surface with some defects was obtained on steel after immersion in blank solution for 2 h. After immersion 8 h, the surface became rougher and more defects, illustrating that the steel was seriously damaged by corrosive medium. After adding 10 mg/L of primordial dopamine, the corrosion sign did not improve significantly after 8 h immersion. With the increase of primordial dopamine (PD) amount, the surface corrosion sign was slightly suppressed. Compared with 8 h immersion, the steel surface exhibited a lot of corrosion pits after 2 h immersion. For modified dopamine (MD), there were only a few large corrosion pits on the steel surface at 10 mg/L and 8 h. When the inhibitor content was 100 mg/L, the corrosion feature of steel surface was greatly inhibited. At the immersion time of 2 h, the size and number of corrosion pits were obviously higher than that at 8 h, explaining that the extension of immersion time was beneficial to metal protection. By EDS analysis, the metal surface mainly contained C, N, O and Cl elements in addition to Fe. N was from the dopamine and Cl came from the corrosion solution.

In acid environment, the corrosion situation was different from the neutral condition. Some corrosion pores were detected on surface after immersion 2 h in blank solution. With the increase of immersion time (8 h), the number of corrosion pore was sharply increased, implying that the metal was seriously attacked by corrosion ions. After adding 10 mg/L of primordial dopamine, the formation of corrosion pore was restrained, but some corrosion grooves were observed. As the primordial dopamine content increased, the number of groove did not decrease. Meanwhile, large corrosion areas were found on the steel surface, implying poor inhibition effect. In case of modified dopamine, the number of corrosion pore at 10 mg/L and 8 h was greatly reduced. When the inhibitor content was 100 mg/L, no obvious corrosion signs were observed, suggesting the best protection result. In the meantime, the corrosion situation of steel at 100 mg/L and 2 h was worse than that at 8 h and slighter than that at 10 mg/L, showing that the change of inhibitor content was more sensitive to the protective effect. In addition, the EDS result was similar with neutral environment. Compared with NaCl solution, the Cl content was higher and the N content was lower on the steel surface after immersion in HCl solution.

Fig. 9 revealed the 3D morphology and roughness (Ra) of the immersed bare steel. In acid solution, the steel surface was coarse, the Ra was 2.18 μm after 2 h immersion in blank solution. After 8 h immersion, the steel surface became more uneven, the Ra increased to 2.73 μm. After adding 10 mg/L of primordial dopamine, the surface became smoother, the Ra decreased to 1.21 μm. At 100 mg/L, the uneven area was further reduced, the Ra decreased to 0.85 μm. Compared with the result after 8 h immersion, the Ra was increased by 0.06 μm after 2 h immersion. In the terms of modified dopamine, except for a few bumps, most of the steel surface was relatively smooth after 8 h immersion in 10 mg/L of inhibitor and the Ra was 0.56 μm, which was greatly lower than primordial dopamine at the same conditions. As the inhibitor content increased to 100 mg/L, the surface became smooth and flat, the Ra was further decreased to 0.37 μm. At 100 mg/L and 2 h, the Ra was 0.44 μm, which was higher than that at 8 h and lower than that at10 mg/L.

In neutral environment, a large number of bumps were detected on surface after immersion 2 h in blank solution and the Ra was 1.41 μm, which was lower than that in acid environment. As the immersion time increased to 8 h, the Ra was obviously increased, implying that the surface was corroded seriously. After adding 10 mg/L of primordial dopamine, no obvious change was observed on the surface and the Ra was similar to the sample in blank solution. As the primordial dopamine content increased, the Ra began to decrease. After 2 h and 8 h immersion in 100 mg/L of primordial dopamine solution, the Ra was about 0.72 μm and 0.63 μm, respectively. For modified dopamine, at 10 mg/L and 8 h, the surface was smooth and the Ra was significantly reduced compared with blank solution. When the inhibitor content was 100 mg/L, the Ra reduced to 0.23 μm, which was 78.3% lower than that at 10 mg/L. Furthermore, the Ra of steel at 100 mg/L and 2 h was similar to the result at 8 h and 100 mg/L, suggesting that the inhibitor concentration played an important role to affect the Ra of steel.

The corrosion rate of bare steel was summarized in Fig. 10 . In acid solution, the corrosion rate was about 2.1 × 10−3 g/cm·h after immersion 8 h in blank solution. After adding primordial dopamine, the corrosion rate was slightly reduced. After increasing the content, the corrosion rate revealed a downtrend, but the value was higher than 1.2 × 10−3 g/cm·h. However, the corrosion rate of steel in modified dopamine solution was lower than that in primordial dopamine solution. At different immersion time, the variation of corrosion rate with and without inhibitor was different. With the increase of immersion time, the corrosion rate in blank solution exhibited an uptrend, while an opposite change trend was detected after adding primordial dopamine and modified dopamine. Meanwhile, the curve of corrosion rate in modified dopamine solution was below the original dopamine. In neutral environment, the variation situation was similar. The corrosion rate was about 0.85 × 10−3 g/cm·h after immersion 8 h in blank solution and showed a downtrend after adding primordial dopamine and modified dopamine. At 100 mg/L, the corrosion rate of steel reduced to 0.56 × 10−3 and 0.16 × 10−3 g/cm·h after adding primordial dopamine and modified dopamine. By comparison, the reduction degree caused by modified dopamine was larger than the primordial dopamine at the same condition. With the increase of immersion time, the corrosion rate was relatively stable in blank solution, which was about 0.8 × 10−3 g/cm·h. Nevertheless, the corrosion rate was slightly reduced after adding primordial dopamine and greatly decreased after adding modified dopamine.

Fig. 11 displayed the adsorption isotherm during corrosion process, which was obtained from equation (3) [42]:
(3)
θ
1 − θ
=
K
a d s
C

Therein, θ, C, and K ads represented surface coverage, inhibitor concentration, adsorption equilibrium constant, respectively. By analysis, the fitted degrees of steel in corrosion solution with and without inhibitor were 0.9476, 0.9778, 0.9586 and 0.9946, implying the high conformity accuracy for Langmuir adsorption. Moreover, the adsorption free energy (ΔG 0 ads) was obtained from equation (4) [43]:
(4)
Δ
G
a d s
0
= − R T I n
(
1000
K
a d s
)

In this case, R was 8.314 J/mol−1K−1 and the T was 298 K. In general, the K ads value could be used to measure the strength of adsorption. Obviously, the K ads value in acid and neutral environments after adding primordial dopamine was about 7.64 and 44.60 L/g, respectively. However, the value was 91.46 and 184.50 L/g after adding modified dopamine in corresponding solution. By contrast, the adsorption strength of primordial dopamine was clearly lower than that of modified dopamine. Meanwhile, neutral environment was more conducive to the dopamine adsorption compared with the acidic environment. Furthermore, the nature of adsorption could be judged by ΔG 0 ads. The nature was physical effect if ΔG 0 ads > −20 kJ/mol and was chemical effect if ΔG 0 ads < −40 kJ/mol. Therefore, the adsorption nature of primordial dopamine and modified dopamine in acid and neutral solutions was physicochemical interaction [20,35], and the anti-corrosion mechanism schematic of modified dopamine was shown in Fig. 12 .

An efficient and green inhibitor was acquired by the reaction between dopamine and polyethylene glycol diacrylate. The corrosion behavior of steel in acid and neutral environments after adding primordial dopamine and modified dopamine was researched. As the inhibitor content increased, the |Z|0.1Hz and IE increased, the i corr and corrosion rate decreased. At the same time, the modified dopamine exhibited a better protective effect in acid and neutral solutions compared with primordial dopamine. Through analysis, the protection behavior of primordial dopamine and modified dopamine was different. For primordial dopamine, the |Z|0.1Hz in acid and neutral solutions after adding 100 mg/L inhibitor was enhanced by 2.23 and 6.61 times compared with bare steel. However, at the same condition, the values were changed to 46.83 and 18.88 times after adding modified dopamine. In addition, the corrosion rate calculated from weight loss test was reduced one order of magnitude after adding modified dopamine, which was obviously lower than primordial dopamine. This was due to the physicochemical interaction of modified dopamine on the steel surface, which was confirmed by adsorption isotherm.

This article was completed with the joint efforts of several authors. In detail, Haolin Xie and Mengxue Xiao are responsible for Writing - original draft; Fan Zhang and Shuilin Ouyang are responsible for Data curation and Writing - review & editing; Y.W. Ye and H. Chen are responsible for Supervision, Validation and Funding acquisition. All data are authentic and reliable. It has not been published in other journals or submitted for publication.

ce:conflict-of-interest.ce:para: The authors declare that they have no known competing financial interests or personal relationships that could have appeared to influence the work reported in this paper.

ce:acknowledgment.ce:para: This work was financially supported by National Natural Science Foundation of China (52101086), Jiangxi Provincial Natural Science Foundation (20224BAB214013), Program of Qingjiang Excellent Young Talents, Jiangxi University of Science and Technology (JXUSTQJYX2020010); Jiangxi Province ‘‘Double Thousand Plan” Innovation Leading Talent Short-term Project (jxsq2020007); Bureau of Science and Technology of Ganzhou Municipality; Distinguished Professor Program of Jinggang Scholars in institutions of higher learning, Jiangxi Province; Jiangxi Province ‘‘Double Thousand Plan” Science and Technology Innovation high-end Talent Project (jxsq2019201039).

ce:section.ce:label: Appendix A
ce:section.ce:para:
The following is the Supplementary data to this article:
Multimedia component 1 Multimedia component 1

ce:section.ce:label: Appendix A
ce:section.ce:para: Supplementary data to this article can be found online at https://doi.org/10.1016/j.matchemphys.2022.127231.
